blocks.content: So far, he has managed to hold on with just his physical abilities, but as his competitors' levels rise, the gap will inevitably widen.
Therefore, even though the school is aware of Rian's situation, they have not been able to create an exception for him.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: We will start the evaluation with the first group. Groups two and three, come out and warm up, the rest can rest.
The cadets in the first group glared as they headed to the training ground.
Kuan, holding the evaluation sheet, signaled the start, and the cadets engaged in a fierce hand-to-hand combat with monstrous shouts.
As Kuan focused on the match, the cadets relaxed.
As always, Tess came to sit next to Rian.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: It was good to talk to Rian before the evaluation, like being alone with a lover on a battlefield.
But Tess's small pleasure was ruined by Bawio's approach.

blocks.speaker: Bawio
blocks.content: Hey, Rian. How about it? Do you think you can avoid last place this time?

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Mind your own business. It's not your concern.

blocks.speaker: Bawio
blocks.content: Haha! Of course. I don't care about last place. But I suddenly had an interesting thought. It might be related to you.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: What are you talking about?

blocks.speaker: Bawio
blocks.content: How about a match with me? Wrestling. Who knows? If you beat the strongest, the school might recognize you. You have to try everything to avoid last place, right?

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Rian turned his gaze away, uninterested.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: I don't need anyone's recognition. I'll fight anyone if I have to, but I don't want to show off useless strength.

blocks.speaker: Bawio
blocks.content: Hmph. Backing out because it's unfavorable?

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: But I'll tell you this.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Even if you kill me in this match, I won't resent you.

blocks.speaker: Bawio
blocks.content: Haha! Big words. Do you think I can't do it?
A thud was heard from the training ground.
Rian and Bawio turned simultaneously.
Tess had thrown Godim to the ground, holding his arm.

blocks.speaker: Godim
blocks.content: I lost. It was a good match.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Phew. You did well too. The last move was a gamble.
She had used the opponent's pushing force to attempt a throw.
If she had misjudged, she would have been in a helpless position, but Tess, with her sensory Schema, couldn't miss the opponent's center of gravity shift.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Hehe. I'm done. Now it's vacation time.
But the atmosphere was different from before.
Rian's expression was colder, and Bawio's eyes were filled with hostility.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Rian, why the face? Bawio, what happened?

blocks.speaker: Bawio
blocks.content: Why ask me? I didn't do anything.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Tess looked at Bawio with suspicion but had no choice but to sit down.
She knew he was lying, but Rian wouldn't answer even if she asked.
When the fifteenth group's match started, Rian, who was in the seventeenth group, entered the training ground to warm up.
Bawio, pretending to follow, turned to Tess and said,

blocks.speaker: Bawio
blocks.content: Tess. You'll soon see how useless Rian is. And how great I am.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: What nonsense? No matter what you do, you'll never look good to me.

blocks.speaker: Bawio
blocks.content: That's harsh. We're friends, after all. You know, Rian and I made a bet earlier.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: A bet? What kind of bet?

blocks.speaker: Bawio
blocks.content: The loser of this match gives up on you. I thought you should know.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Bawio, Rian would never gamble with someone else. That's the difference between you and Rian.

blocks.speaker: Bawio
blocks.content: Really? Just watch.
The match was set, so it didn't matter.
Seeing Rian humiliated in front of the cadets would embarrass Tess too.
Wrestling was perfect for this. It allowed him to slowly torment his opponent.
When it was the turn of Group 17, Rian and Bawio faced each other in the arena.
Comparing their bare bodies, the difference in size was almost 1.5 times.
Although Rian was not small for his age, Bawio's bulk was so intimidating that it looked like a match between an adult and a child.
The cadets fixed their eyes on the two, like people expecting to witness a brutal feast in the wild.

blocks.speaker: Bawio
blocks.content: Shall we start? Are you ready?

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Anytime. Since it has come to this, let's have a good match.
As Rian and Bawio took their wrestling stances, Kuan put down his notes and focused on their match.
As soon as the signal to start was given, the two collided.
There was a loud bang.
It was a sound that made one worry that a skull might have been broken.
Kuan didn't consider it a foul.
Collisions to close the distance were common.
Rian wasn't unprepared, but when their heads collided, sparks flew before his eyes, and his upper body was thrown back.
In such collisions, weight was everything.
This was why Bawio had bulked up so desperately.
As Rian staggered, Bawio immediately followed up, grabbing the back of Rian's neck.
Rian quickly regained his composure and did the same.
They stood like two bulls locking horns.
Their faces turned red as they held their breath.
Gradually, Rian's legs began to drag on the ground as he was pushed back.
Once again, weight was the deciding factor.
Huuuuh...
Bawio's breath escaped through his teeth.
He had heard the rumors, but facing Rian in person was shocking.
Rian couldn't use Schema.
Normally, he should have been knocked out in the first collision.
